---
title: "Risyad Abiyyu Siregar"
subtitle: "dr., MPH, DIC · Medical Doctor & Global Health Professional"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: assets/images/about-profile.JPEG
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: "mailto:abiyyusiregar@gmail.com"
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: "https://www.linkedin.com/in/abiyyusiregar/"
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: "https://github.com/rasiregar"
---

To live a dignified life. This has been my life motto for so long. I am on a mission to achieve that vision, for me and for everyone around.

Medical doctor and global health professional with experience spanning clinical medicine, research, and international health policy. Experienced in synthesising complex scientific evidence into clear policy-relevant insights for international health programmes, technical advisory processes, and decision-makers.

---

<details>
<summary>**Education**</summary>

<div>

**Imperial College London** · London, United Kingdom

*Master of Public Health (MPH)* · September 2023 – September 2024

- Fully funded by the Indonesia Endowment Fund for Education (LPDP) scholarship
- Specialisation: Global Health
- Dissertation: *Global Economic Cost of Malaria: A Systematic Review and Quantitative Analysis of Available Evidence*
- Grade: **Distinction** (74.25/100)
- Awards: Faculty of Medicine Dean's Prize for the Highest Overall Performance (MPH Cohort 23/24); Joint Best Dissertation for the Master of Public Health Course Award

**Universitas Indonesia** · Jakarta, Indonesia

*Doctor of Medicine (dr.)* · August 2016 – April 2022

- Thesis: *Sensitivity profile of Malassezia spp. towards fluconazole and miconazole*
- Grade: **Cum laude** (3.61/4)

</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary>**Work Experience**</summary>

<div>

**Oxford University Clinical Research Unit Indonesia** · Jakarta, Indonesia

*Scientific and Technical Officer* · June 2025 – present

- Support implementation of a large-scale digital health intervention trial (SPHERES) across two pilot regencies, strengthening primary healthcare information systems serving over 2 million people.
- Coordinate with the Indonesian Ministry of Health, district health offices, and frontline healthcare workers to align digital reporting systems with national health strategies.
- Conduct mixed-methods health systems research to identify operational barriers and enabling factors for sustainable integration of digital health tools in primary care services.
- Analyse maternal and child health indicators using integrated datasets from national health information systems (ePuskesmas, SIKDA, Sigizi).

---

**World Health Organization** · Geneva, Switzerland

*Intern – One Health & Neglected Tropical Diseases Programme* · December 2024 – May 2025

- Supported WHO efforts to operationalise the One Health approach for neglected zoonotic diseases, including rabies, cystic echinococcosis, taeniasis/cysticercosis, and foodborne trematodiases.
- Collaborated with WHO technical teams and external partners including Gavi and US CDC to develop tools supporting integration of rabies post-exposure prophylaxis into national immunisation programmes.
- Supported the Diagnostics Technical Advisory Group (DTAG) through evidence synthesis and drafting of WHO Target Product Profiles for parasitic disease diagnostics.
- Assisted preparation and coordination of the 2nd WHO Global Skin-Related NTD Meeting (2025) involving more than 300 international participants.
- Developed a routine data insights dashboard for NTD surveillance using Power BI and R.

---

**Evidence Based Health Policy Center, IMERI FKUI** · Jakarta, Indonesia

*Research Associate (Part-time)* · August 2024 – May 2025

- Conducted qualitative research evaluating Indonesia's health service delivery using the WHO Health System Performance Assessment framework.
- Applied thematic and SCAT analysis to synthesise stakeholder perspectives into policy-relevant insights.

---

**Baa General Hospital, Rote Ndao Regency** · East Nusa Tenggara, Indonesia

*General Practitioner* · August 2022 – August 2023

- Provided frontline clinical care in a government-designated underserved district hospital in eastern Indonesia.
- Initiated and implemented a community-based tuberculosis screening programme, increasing TB case detection eightfold through outreach and community engagement.
- Collaborated with local health authorities to strengthen disease surveillance in resource-constrained settings.

---

**Clinical Epidemiology and Evidence-Based Medicine (CEEBM) Unit, Cipto Mangunkusumo Hospital** · Jakarta, Indonesia

*Research Intern* · January 2022 – July 2022

- Conducted a systematic review on Comprehensive Geriatric Assessment.
- Contributed to a COVID-19 vaccine immunogenicity study among healthcare workers.
- Assisted early-stage Health Technology Assessment analyses for the Indonesian Ministry of Health.

</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary>**Publications**</summary>

<div>

[Report of the eighteenth meeting of the Strategic and Technical Advisory Group for Neglected Tropical Diseases](https://www.who.int/publications/i/item/9789240112285)

*World Health Organization, Global Neglected Tropical Diseases Programme* · August 2025

---

[WHO guidelines for the treatment of patients with cystic echinococcosis](https://www.who.int/publications/i/item/9789240110472)

*World Health Organization, Global Neglected Tropical Diseases Programme* · June 2025

---

[The effectiveness of comprehensive geriatric assessment intervention for older people in outpatient setting: a systematic review/meta-analysis](https://doi.org/10.1186/s12877-025-06048-8)

Eka Dian Safitri, Respati Wulansari Ranakusuma, Natan Kevin Partogu Siagian, Jessica Marsigit, Siti Rizny Fitriana Saldi, Widyaningsih Widyaningsih, Rahmi Istanti, Muhammad Khifzhon Azwar, **Risyad Abiyyu Siregar**, Kuntjoro Harimurti, Siti Setiati · *BMC Geriatrics* · July 2025

---

Indonesia Diabetes Diagnosis, Treatment, and Control: Trends, Challenges, and Socioeconomic Influences (2013–2022)

Farizal Rizky Muharram, **Risyad Abiyyu Siregar**, Mohammad Qoimam Bilqisthi Zulfikar, Aqsha Azhary Nur, Dante Saksono Harbuwono · *Preprints for The Lancet: Diabetes and Endocrinology* · October 2024 *(awaiting peer review)*

---

Evaluating Indonesia's Health Service Delivery: A Qualitative Study Using the WHO Health System Performance Assessment Framework

Mohammad Kurniawan, Nico Gamalliel, **Risyad Abiyyu Siregar**, Deandra Ardya, Ahmad Fuady · *Presented at European Health Management Association (EHMA) Conference 2025, Rennes, France* · June 2025

</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary>**Core Competencies**</summary>

<div>

**Medical & Scientific Expertise**

Clinical medicine (MD) with specialisation in infectious diseases and global health · Zoonotic diseases and operationalisation of One Health approaches · Evidence-based medicine, diagnostics, and health systems strengthening · Real-world evidence (RWE) and digital health transformation in primary care

**Scientific Communication & Stakeholder Engagement**

Scientific writing and technical report drafting · Policy brief development and evidence translation for decision-makers · Cross-sectoral collaboration with international organisations, ministries of health, and local health authorities

**Research & Data Analysis**

Quantitative analysis using R and epidemiological methods · Qualitative research including thematic analysis and SCAT · Systematic reviews, meta-analysis, and evidence synthesis (Covidence, ASReview) · Data visualisation and analytical reporting (Power BI, R Shiny, Excel)

**Project Implementation & Coordination**

Multisite stakeholder coordination across district health offices and community health systems · Secretariat and coordination support for international technical meetings · Technical support for programme implementation in resource-constrained settings · Monitoring and evaluation including dashboard development

**Languages**

English — professional fluency (IELTS 8.0 / CEFR C1) · Indonesian — native

</div>
</details>
